title: Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)
content:

'''''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire''''' is an action/adventure third/first-person shooter game set between the time of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']].

In conjunction with other [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'']] releases, the game was released on the Nintendo 64 platform in [[1996]]. A year later, in [[1997]], it was released on the PC platform for Microsoft Windows.

==Plot summary==
The game is divided in four parts (or chapters). The first chapter takes place during the beginning of ''The Empire Strikes Back''. [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] is trapped on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] when the Imperial forces attack. As the player, you have to pilot a snowspeeder in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] destroying [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]], [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] and [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Imperial probes]]. Then you must fight your way through [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] back to your ship the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']]. After escaping Hoth you must fight your way through the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]] and the several waves of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] before escaping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].

In the second chapter, Dash tracks down some of the bounty hunters that had been dispatched to capture [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Fighting his way through several trains and finally confronting [[IG-88D/Legends|IG-88]] on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] and assaulting Imperial outposts throughout canyons, on [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] to finally find and attack [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].

In the third chapter, Boba Fett has escaped and the player's mission changes: you must now protect [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] from an assassination attempt ordered by the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun's]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], by fighting a deadly gang of [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bikers through [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] and [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]]. Dash then recovers Imperial [[Death Star II plans|secret plans]] of the second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]], that are being carried aboard the freighter ''[[Suprosa]]''.

Finally, in the last chapter, Dash, along with Luke and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], travels to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] to rescue [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] from Prince Xizor's stronghold by infiltrating Xizor's palace through the sewers below [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]], fighting their way through the castle and eventually engaging in a space battle above the planet. The game's greatest surprise comes at the end, when Dash flies within Xizor's space station to destroy it. In the game the player is led to believe that Rendar perishes in the blast, with a secret ending on harder difficulties revealing he secretly survived; however in the book Rendar does not participate in the stations destruction, remaining outside in his ship with the rest of the fleet.

==Gameplay==
===Part I: The Battle of Hoth===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=The Battle of Hoth
|text=It is a time of crisis. Imperial
probes, scouring the galaxy for
the hidden Rebel base, have
discovered their outpost on the
remote ice world of Hoth.

Darth Vader, obsessed with
finding Luke Skywalker, has
sent his forces to engage
the Rebels in battle.

With the dreaded Imperial
fleet approaching, a lone
freighter has entered the Hoth
system…}}

====Battle of Hoth====
Here, the player is introduced with the famous [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], and this is the only time the player will use the [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeder]] in the game.

*Story: [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], captain of the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']] and mercenary-for-hire, has arrived with a supply shipment of assault blasters for [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]]. After landing he meets up with his long time friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. The two could not have met at a more opportune time. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] is on its way and the Rebels could use another good pilot. Dash has never been one to say no to a good dogfight. Han talks to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and gets Dash a spot in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].
*Objectives: Destroy [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Imperial probe droids]], [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]], and [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]], giving the Alliance the time they need to evacuate Echo Base.

====Escape from Echo Base====
[[File:RendarEchoBase-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Dash runs through the hangars of Echo Base as the ''Millennium Falcon'' takes off.]]
The [[Wampa/Legends|wampas]] make their first appearance of the game here. This level introduces the game's most numerous 3rd/1st person shooting levels, along with its first boss: an Imperial AT-ST.

*Story: While Rogue Squadron has been able to give most of the Rebels adequate time to escape, they have not been entirely successful. The main generator has been destroyed and Imperial troops have entered the base. Han, Leia and Luke have escaped, but Dash is left behind. With the generator down, the shield to [[Bay 3]] (where the ''Outrider'' is docked) is closed. If Dash is going to get off Hoth alive, he had better find a way to open that bay door!
*Objectives: Locate and activate the emergency generators, then make it to the ''Outrider''. Watch out for [[Wampa/Legends|wampas]]! They can be either friend or foe.

====Asteroid Chase====
This level introduces the flying aspect of the game, where players take control of the ''Outrider's'' gun turrets to destroy asteroids and TIE Fighters.

*Story: Dash has escaped Hoth in one piece, but he's not quite in the clear. An [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] has been spotted just off Hoth. As if things weren't bad enough, the ''Outrider'' is approaching an [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]. With his co-pilot droid [[LE-BO2D9|Leebo]] at the helm, Dash mans the gun turrets to fend off incoming TIE Fighters and Bombers. Only when the TIEs are destroyed can the ''Outrider'' make the jump to lightspeed and escape the Empire.
*Objectives: Man the ''Outrider's'' gunnery controls and destroy incoming TIEs and asteroids. Shoot the red asteroids for challenge points!

===Part II: In Search of Boba Fett===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=In Search of Boba Fett
|text=A rival bounty hunter has forced
Boba Fett into hiding before he
could deliver his prize, the
carbonite-frozen Han Solo, to the
vile gangster, Jabba the Hutt.

As Luke Skywalker and rogue
mercenary DASH RENDAR search for
Boba Fett, a sinister new figure
emerges from the underworld.

This cunning and ruthless head
of the powerful crime syndicate
BLACK SUN plots to ensnare young
Skywalker and replace Lord Vader
as the Emperor's right hand…}}

====Ord Mantell Junkyard====
[[File:RendarOrdMantell-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Dash rides the trains through the junkyards of [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]].]]
Another running around mission, made much more difficult with the addition of a narrow moving train and a difficult boss: [[IG-88D/Legends|IG-88]].

*Story: After escaping the asteroid field, Dash discovers that [[IG-88D/Legends|IG-88]], the bounty hunter trailing Boba Fett, is on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]. He's in a junkyard looking for parts to repair his ship after it was damaged by Boba Fett. Dash has landed on Ord Mantell hoping to [[Mission to Ord Mantell (Dash Rendar)|track down]] IG-88 and find out where Boba Fett is hiding. He knows that Princess Leia would pay handsomely for news of Han's whereabouts.
*Objectives: Move from train car to train car until you reach the end. Watch out for turns in the track! Jumping while the train is turning can be dangerous! A magnetic field surrounding the train disables all of Dash's weapons, except his blaster. He will be able to access other weapons once he reaches IG-88.
*This level is notable for its use of the "Hyperspace" music from the Empire Strikes Back soundtrack that was edited and looped.

====Gall Spaceport====
[[File:RendarSmugglersCanyon-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Dash rides a jetpack through the canyons of Gall.]]
The [[Wampa/Legends|wampas]] make their second appearance of this game here [[Cliff wampa|appearing brown]]. This level introduces the jetpack and pits the player against three bosses, an Imperial AT-ST, Boba Fett and the [[Slave I/Legends|Slave I]].

*Story: Before destroying IG-88, Dash finds out where Boba Fett is hiding; he's sought refuge at the Imperial moon base on [[Gall/Legends|Gall]]. With the Empire on his tail, the only way Dash can get into the base is on foot through the back entrance in [[Smuggler's Canyon]]. Once inside the base, Dash hopes to locate Boba Fett and reach Solo before it's too late.
*Objectives: Infiltrate the Imperial base, locate the jetpack and reach Boba Fett before he has a chance to escape.

===Part III: Hunting the Assassins===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Hunting the Assassins
|text=The Rebels locate Boba Fett, but
with help from an Imperial escort,
the bounty hunter escapes from Gall.

Above the planet, an assassin
nearly kills Luke. Princess Leia,
fearing for his safety, asks Dash
to follow him to Tatooine.

There, at the abandoned home of
Obi-Wan Kenobi, Luke refines his
Jedi skills while Dash keeps an
eye out for trouble in a familiar
Mos Eisley cantina…}}

====Mos Eisley and Beggar's Canyon====
[[File:RendarTatooine-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Dash rushes against swoop riders at dangerous velocities.]]
The player is challenged with another variety in the form of a race/chasing stage, and they must navigate narrow paths at breakneck speeds or face death. The [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]] also makes its only appearance in this game during this level.

*Story: While hiding out in Mos Eisley, Dash overhears a discussion. Jabba the Hutt has hired a swoop gang to kill Skywalker. The mercenaries speed away towards Kenobi's old home. Why does Jabba the Hutt want Skywalker dead? Dash had better reach Luke before the swoop gang does, or he may never find out!
*Objectives: Race through the streets of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] and [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]]. You must destroy the other swoop bikers by ramming them into walls or obstacles, then get to Kenobi's place at the end of the canyon. Watch your speed! If you go too fast you might crash! On the other hand, you need to go really fast to clear the [[Stone Needle/Legends|Eye of the Needle]].
*Trivia: The stage will take place during the day in both Easy, Medium, & Hard modes, however, only in Jedi mode, the stage will take place at night. Also, there is a short-cut that can be taken to avoid most of the Swoop Gang members all together and finish the stage in record time.

====Imperial Freighter ''Suprosa''====
[[File:SuprosaStormtrooper-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Fighting Stormtroopers on the ''Suprosa''.]]
Back on his feet, Dash must make his way through strange terrains consisting of hallways, offices and engine rooms, finally ending with a duel with the level's boss: the [[Imperial loader droid]]. Although the level's icon depicts Dash wearing his jetpack, it is not actually featured in this level.

*Story: With the swoop gang thwarted, Dash meets up with Luke. He tells Luke that Leia is meeting with Black Sun to find out who wants him dead. Spies from Black Sun recently gave Luke the location of a [[Suprosa|freighter]] carrying [[Death Star II plans|secret Imperial construction plans]]. Dash warns Luke to stay away from Black Sun, but Luke knows that he can't afford not to check it out.
*Objectives: Dash must make his way through the freighter and find the supercomputer containing the secret plans.

===Part IV: Lair of the Dark Prince===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Lair of the Dark Prince
|text=Princess Leia has met with the
mysterious Dark Prince Xizor.
When she discovers his plan, she
is held captive deep within his
lair beneath Imperial City.

Xizor is unaware that both Darth
Vader and the Rebels have learned
of his treachery and race to
confront him.

Determined to rescue Leia and
destroy Black Sun, Luke, Chewie,
Lando and Dash slip unnoticed into
the depths of Imperial City's
underworld…}}

====Sewers of Imperial City====
[[File:RendarSewersOfCoruscant-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Dash enters the sewers of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with his handy jetpack.]]
The jetpack returns for this level and is put to good use as Dash must navigate through the maze of pipes and creepy [[Dianoga/Legends|dianogas]], including the boss: the Giant Dianoga.

*Story: Following Xizor's advice, Emperor Palpatine has allowed the plans for the new Death Star that were on the freighter to fall into Rebel hands. While the Alliance works to decode the plans, Luke, Chewie, Lando and Dash travel to Coruscant to locate Leia. Dash must travel alone through the [[Sewers of Coruscant|sewer system]] and find a way into the palace.
*Objectives: Help Dash get through the sewers to get to the entrance of Xizor's palace. Watch out for the [[Dianoga/Legends|dianogas]] that lurk underwater!

====Xizor's Palace====
This strange level forces the player to negotiate poor building planning with the jetpack, as the entire level is teeming with enemies and the final boss: the [[Black Sun gladiator droid|Gladiator droid]].

*Story: Dash has now penetrated [[Xizor's Palace]]. Leebo informs Dash that there is a way to cut off Xizor's skyhook space fortress from the palace.
*Objectives: Place [[pulse bomb/Legends|explosives]] on the service panels to the space elevator that connects Xizor's palace to the skyhook in orbit.

====Skyhook Battle====
[[File:OutriderBattleOverCoruscant-SotE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The ''Outrider'' engages in a dogfight with several [[StarViper-class attack platform/Legends|Star Vipers]].]]
The final level ends with the [[Battle over Coruscant|dogfight]] over Coruscant.

*Story: Imperial and Rebel forces have descended upon Xizor's skyhook. X-wings, TIE Fighters and Xizor's Star Vipers fill the sky. The skyhook must be destroyed.
*Objectives: Dash must destroy Xizor's fleet, penetrate the skyhook's reactor, destroy the core and try to make it out in one piece.

==Development==
The ''Shadows of the Empire'' project began in January [[1995]]. The development of the project coincided with Nintendo offering [[LucasArts]] to produce one of its first games for the upcoming [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] console, boasting real-time action in an interactive 3d environment. The developers opted for such an interactive game instead of the CD-based pre-rendered multimedia productions such as the earlier ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''. As the release of that console was expected in late July, the first N64 games, such as ''Shadows'', were developed inside an emulator run on an [[Wikipedia:SGI Onyx|SGI Onyx]].<ref name="shadows">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref>

[[Mark Haigh-Hutchinson]] was the project leader. Art direction was based on concept drawings by [[Doug Chiang]] and [[Michael Butkus]] who made art for the whole project; [[Jon Knoles]] developed further looks for the protagonist Dash Rendar and the villain Prince Xizor. [[Paul Topolos]] created concept and storyboard art with dialogues written by Knoles.<ref name="shadows" />

A story outline was ready by [[April 17]], [[1995]] which contained details such as the Battle of Hoth,<ref name="wargames">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 4: Battle Zones, III. War Games</ref> the mission to Ord Mantell and infiltration of ''Suprosa''.<ref name="bikers" /> The designers decided to include a playable version of the Battle of Hoth from ''Empire Strikes Back'', as it would be the first time a gamer would be able to fly real-time around and between Imperial Walkers (the previous appearance in ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' was a [[Wikipedia:interactive movie|cinematic]] [[Wikipedia:rail shooter|rail shooter]] without offering any real gameplay); it would also portray Rendar as heroic, fighting for the Rebel cause.<ref name="wargames" />

In order for the new starships to be included in the game, Jon Knoles had to design schematics derived from Doug Chiang's one-dimensional sketches, from which the digital models were to be built. A schematic of the ''Outrider'' was completed on [[February 22]] [[1995]]. The N64 engine could handle 4,000 polygons per frame in real time, and in order to allow for many ships to appear in the same scene, they tried to limit the detail to less than 100 polygons; the exception was the ''Millennium Falcon'', which, due to its complex design, had to be made of 250 polygons. The ''Falcon'' and the ''Outrider'', being assymetrical and round, were the hardest to design.<ref name="Battleships" />

The team used half a dozen software tools to design the spaceships. Each ship model had three versions of varying degree of detail; a ship seen far away would not necessitate a lot of detail, so a Star Destroyer might consist simply of two triangles, thus saving resources and allowing more objects to appear in the scene. According to the distance, the same ship would change to its middle-distance model or the close-up model. One of the challenges the programmers faced was making the transition seem smooth as the camera approached an object.<ref name="Battleships">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 4: Battle Zones, II. Battleships</ref>

[[Andy Holdun]] designed the 3d models of starships with Knoles and [[Chris Hockabout]] drawing some surface textures for the 3d objects. Hockabout made use of [[Wikipedia:Deluxe Paint Animation|D Paint Animator]] and its use of "cells" in order to collect the textures in one file. They also utilized another graphics program with a library of high resolution texture files that could be lighted from different angles. Some ship models utilize photographic reference from physical models for texture, such as the ''Falcon''. A small statue of Dash was made by LucasArts, whose dimensions and proportions were scanned in order to develop further consistent designs.<ref name="shadows" />

Knoles usually had the basic ideas of the levels which then discussed with ''Dark Forces'' veteran [[Jim Current]], in order to improve action and puzzles; Current designed the environments with [[Wikipedia:AutoCAD|AutoCAD]]. Unlike the earlier [[Jedi Engine]], which didn't allow for angled surfaces, the N64 engine allowed more complex forms, such as round arches and polygonal-shaped corridors.<ref name="shadows" />

NPC A.I. was improved in comparison to earlier ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'', with the enemies having somee "awareness" of their surroundings and what they must do during confrontations with the player/Dash Rendar.<ref name="shadows" /> Haigh-Hutchinson, who oversaw the Battle of Hoth-level, programmed the Walkers to be aware of the Rebels's distance, direction, speed, and the laser's speed, and fire it's anticipating shots accordingly. The hostiles's basic A.I. behavior was to balance between predictability and controlled random factors.<ref name="wargames" /> During space battles, the system would be able to show a large amount of ships in the same scenery by shared processing and memory resources, such as different ships sharing the same decisions; following the decisions of a single [[squadron/Legends|squadron]] "leader"; or copied instances of ships elsewhere.<ref name="battle">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 4: Battle Zones, IV. The Final Battle</ref>

An unused matte painting made for ''Return of the Jedi'', showing Luke's X-wing and the ''Millennium Falcon'' parked on Tatooine, was digitised and used for the epilogue cutscene. A digitised layout of Mos Eisley made by [[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM]] for the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] of {{Film|4}} inspired [[Bill Stoneham]] who designed the 3d environment of the town for the Part III of the game.<ref name="shadows" /> ILM produced concept art for the "conehead" swoop drivers for the Special Edition and similar models appear as the enemies in the same level of the game.<ref name="bikers">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 3: Journey Into Darkness, V. Bounty Hunters, Droids and Bikers</ref>

The game decided to establish Dash's survival after the battle of ''Falleen's First'' (whose death is left ambiguous in the novel and the comic), as it would be unfair for the player to have their character killed after their adventure of survival.<ref name="battle" />

===Technical notes===
[[File:Wampanonsense.png|thumb|right|200px|Mynd you, wampa bites Kan be pretty nasti…]]
*The PC version of the game supported a special set of 3D graphics drivers and filters and therefore had very peculiar graphic cards requirements, more than any other LucasArts game of its time. Its video driver requirements have been incompatible with more recent generations of hardware unless patched or run within a PC emulator. More recent versions of Microsoft Virtual PC, VirtualBox, & VMWare Workstation have all dropped the necessary video driver support. The open source PCem emulator includes a compatible 3D accelerated driver.
*The N64 and PC versions of the video game differ greatly. The PC version has sharper and smoother gameplay graphics (using a 3D card) than the N64 version. Both versions have slightly different cutscenes—the PC version has full-motion cinematic cutscenes with sound effects and voices, while the N64 version only has drawn sketches with text at the bottom of the screen.
*On the PC version of the game, on a player's file named "_Credits" (a space before "Credits"), the game credits will play as usual, but with the addition of strange subtitles that parody the "Swedish" subtitles from the opening of [[Wikipedia:Monty Python and the Holy Grail|''Monty Python and the Holy Grail'']].
*In the Nintendo 64 version of ''Shadows'', the supercomputer housing the [[Death Star II plans]], when viewed from a sufficient distance in Dash's perspective, greatly resembles an actual Nintendo 64 game console with a ''Shadows'' cartridge plugged into its slot.

===Music===
Selections from the [[Shadows of the Empire (soundtrack)|''Shadows of the Empire'']] soundtrack score were later used in the game along with a number of cues from John Williams' [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] soundtrack:
*The ''Shadows of the Empire'' Main Title/Title Screen theme for the N64 version is actually an unused cue from ''The Empire Strikes Back'' and can be heard in "[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack|Main Title/The Ice Planet Hoth]]" on the film's soundtrack.
*The level "Battle of Hoth" uses the main cue taken from "[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack|The Battle of Hoth]]".
*For the N64 release, every level where the player controls Dash Rendar on foot will begin with a brief music cue from the [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi (soundtrack)|''Return of the Jedi'' soundtrack]] that can be heard in the track "The Battle of Endor". For both versions, it also plays after Dash regenerates from losing a life while on foot.
*The level "Escape from Echo Base" & the N64 version of "Imperial Freighter ''Suprosa''" use the cue "The Battle of Yavin (Launch from the Fourth Moon)" from the [[Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope Original Soundtrack|''A New Hope'' soundtrack]].
*The "Asteroid Chase" Level and the first stage to the N64 version of "Skyhook Battle" use a cue from "The Asteroid Field".
*The "Ord Mantell Junkyard" level uses the "[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack|Hyperspace]]" cue from the soundtrack.
*The "Gall Spaceport" level uses the "[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack|Departure of Boba Fett]]" cue from the film. Strangely, the track "The Battle of Gall" from the official ''Shadows of the Empire'' soundtrack is never used in-game.
*The "Mos Eisley and Beggar's Canyon" Level uses part of "Beggar's Canyon Chase" from the [[Shadows of the Empire (soundtrack)|''Shadows of the Empire'' soundtrack]].
*The PC version of "Imperial Freighter ''Suprosa''" uses "The Walls Converge" From the ''A New Hope'' Soundtrack.
*The "Sewers of Imperial City" Level uses "Into the Sewers".
*The "Xizor's Palace" level uses the second part of [[Xizor's Theme]].
*With the exception of the AT-ST in "Gall Spaceport", all the bosses use the secondary cue from "The Battle of Hoth".
*The first stage of the PC version of the "Skyhook Battle" level uses a music cue from "Mynock Cave" while stage 2 of both releases use one from the middle of "The Destruction Of Xizor's Palace".
The PC version's disc is a Mixed Mode CD, when it is inserted into a CD player the soundtrack will play. It includes 13 tracks straight from the game. The info according to ripping it shows it's composed by [[John Williams]]. These tracks are not the same ones from the Official Soundtrack, but are somewhat alike.

=== Bonuses ===
For both versions, if you collect all the Challenge Points in all the levels and then beat the final level; you will be rewarded a variety of bonuses depending on the difficulty you beat the game on with each difficulty level provided more bonuses.

==== The bonuses provided are as follows ====

* '''Easy''': [[LE-BO2D9|Leebo]] Scan; Leebo provides a map from a top down point of view showing the layout and objects of interest. (Hold camera for 5 seconds).
* '''Medium''': [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] and [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|Tie Fighter]]; In the Skyhook Battle level during the dogfight, you can change the [[Outrider/Legends|Outrider]] into an X-wing and then a Tie Fighter. (Hold camera for 5 seconds).
* '''Hard''': [[Personal energy shield/Legends|Invincibility]] [[Seeker missile|and]] [[Flamethrower/Legends|all]] [[Pulse cannon/Legends|available]] [[Stunner/Legends|weapons]] [[Disruptor/Legends|enabled]]; You are given an invincibility power up and 100 shots of all the game’s weapons. (Hold crouch for 15 seconds).
* '''Jedi''': Friendly [[Wampa/Legends|Wampas]]; The wampas are now your friends and won’t attack you or each other, only your enemies. (No action needed, simply stand anywhere near a wampa and let it follow you; also works on the [[cliff wampa]]).

==Continuity==
Although the game designers tried not to contradict the novel<ref name="shadows" /> there are many notable differences between the game, novel, and comics.
*Though Dash has only a small supporting role in the comics and novel, he was chosen to be the player character of the game.
*In the novel, Luke recalls that Dash managed to bring down an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. In the game, it's revealed that Dash destroyed three of them.
*Dash Rendar manages to either outrun, or either kill most or all of Jabba's hitmen before they even reach [[Ben Kenobi's home/Legends|Ben Kenobi's hut]].
*He finds Luke Skywalker outside of the hut either standing (N64), or also on a swoop (PC), and he already knows about the ''Suprosa''.
*There is no mention made of the Bothan agents, and Luke and Dash depart for the ''Suprosa'' from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] instead of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]].
*Dash boards the ''Suprosa'' alone, and is forced to fight his way to the supercomputer.
*In the game, Dash plants bombs to destroy Xizor's Palace, while in the novel he joins Luke, Lando and Chewie in infiltrating it. It's [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] who drops a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] down the garbage chute that causes its collapse.
*If the player beats the game on medium difficulty or higher, the game reveals that Dash escaped the skyhook via a jump to hyperspace and decided to lay low for a while, but the scene is only viewable if the player flies the ''Outrider'' out of the skyhook. He remarks that it is good to be known as a martyr while still being alive. Dash's survival is consistent with the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution|sequel]] and other sources.
